[
  {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "Quickest way without any code changes — open the browser console (F12 or ⌥⌘I in Safari/Vivaldi) and run:\n\n```js\nJSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('spark_calc_dev_scenarios'))\n```\n\nThat will expand the full object showing all saved scenarios and their slider values.\n\nTo see just one scenario by name:\n\n```js\nJSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('spark_calc_dev_scenarios'))['your scenario name']\n```\n\n---\n\nIf you want it accessible from the UI without opening DevTools, the simplest addition would be a **View** button that shows the selected scenario's JSON in a native `alert()` — same approach as the prompt, works in all browsers. Want me to add that?"
  }
]

      "old_string": "const STORAGE_KEY = 'spark_calc_prod';\n\nfunction saveState() {\n  try {\n    const state = {};\n    document.querySelectorAll('input[type=range]').forEach(inp => state[inp.id] = inp.value);\n    document.querySelectorAll('input[type=checkbox]').forEach(inp => state[inp.id] = inp.checked);\n    localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(state));\n    const btn = el('btn_save');\n    if (btn) { btn.textContent = 'Saved ✓'; setTimeout(() => btn.textContent = 'Save', 1500); }\n  } catch(e) {}\n}\n\nfunction loadState() {\n  try {\n    const raw = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);\n    if (!raw) return;\n    const state = JSON.parse(raw);\n    if (state.ram_node !== undefined) el('ram_node').value = state.ram_node;\n    document.querySelectorAll('input[type=range]').forEach(inp => {\n      if (state[inp.id] !== undefined) inp.value = state[inp.id];\n    });\n    document.querySelectorAll('input[type=checkbox]').forEach(inp => {\n      if (state[inp.id] !== undefined) inp.checked = (state[inp.id] === true || state[inp.id] === 'true');\n    });\n  } catch(e) {}\n}\n\nfunction resetState() {\n  try { localStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY); } catch(e) {}\n  document.querySelectorAll('input[type=range]').forEach(inp => inp.value = inp.defaultValue);\n  document.querySelectorAll('input[type=checkbox]').forEach(inp => inp.checked = inp.defaultChecked);\n  recalc();\n}\n\n// ─── Initial run\nloadState();\nrecalc();",

JSON.parse(r) : {}; } catch(e) { return {}; }\n}\n\nfunction _putScenarios(s) {\n  try { localStorage.setItem(SCENARIOS_KEY, JSON.stringify(s)); } catch(e) {}\n}\n\nfunction renderScenarioList() {\n  const sel = el('scenario_select');\n  if (!sel) return;\n  const names = Object.keys(_getScenarios()).sort();\n  sel.innerHTML = names.length\n    ? names.map(n => `<option value=\"${n}\">${n}</option>`).join('')\n    : '<option value=\"\" disabled selected>— no saved scenarios —</option>';\n}\n\nfunction saveScenario() {\n  const name = (window.prompt('Scenario name:') || '').trim();\n  if (!name) return;\n  const state = {};\n  document.querySelectorAll('input[type=range]').forEach(inp => state[inp.id] = inp.value);\n  document.querySelectorAll('input[type=checkbox]').forEach(inp => state[inp.id] = inp.checked);\n  const scenarios = _getScenarios();\n  scenarios[name] = state;\n  _putScenarios(scenarios);\n  renderScenarioList();\n  const sel = el('scenario_select');\n  if (sel) sel.value = name;\n  const btn = el('btn_save_scenario');\n  if (btn) { btn.textContent = 'Saved ✓'; setTimeout(() => btn.textContent = 'Save Scenario', 1500); }\n}\n\nfunction loadScenario() {\n  const sel = el('scenario_select');\n  if (!sel || !sel.value) return;\n  const state = _getScenarios()[sel.value];\n  if (!state) return;\n  // restore ram_node first so pre-scale block in recalc() computes correct RAM/VM max\n  if (state.ram_node !== undefined) el('ram_node').value = state.ram_node;\n  document.querySelectorAll('input[type=range]').forEach(inp => {\n    if (state[inp.id] !== undefined) inp.value = state[inp.id];\n  });\n  document.querySelectorAll('input[type=checkbox]').forEach(inp => {\n    if (state[inp.id] !== undefined) inp.checked = (state[inp.id] === true || state[inp.id] === 'true');\n  });\n  recalc();\n  // localStorage intentionally not written — load is explore-only until user clicks Save\n  const btn = el('btn_load_scenario');\n  if (btn) { btn.textContent = 'Loaded ✓'; setTimeout(() => btn.textContent = 'Load', 1500); }\n}\n\nfunction viewScenario() {\n  const sel = el('scenario_select');\n  if (!sel || !sel.value) { alert('Select a scenario from the dropdown first.'); return; }\n  const state = _getScenarios()[sel.value];\n  if (!state) return;\n  const w = window.open('', '_blank', 'width=540,height=640,resizable=yes,scrollbars=yes');\n  w.document.write(`<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><title>Scenario JSON — ${sel.value}</title>\n<style>\n  body { margin:0; background:#0f1117; color:#e0e0e0; font-family:monospace; font-size:13px; }\n  h2 { margin:0; padding:16px 20px 10px; font-size:13px; letter-spacing:0.08em; text-transform:uppercase; color:#b482ff; border-bottom:1px solid #2a2a3a; }\n  pre { margin:0; padding:20px; white-space:pre-wrap; word-break:break-all; line-height:1.6; }\n</style></head><body>\n<h2>Scenario JSON Object — ${sel.value}</h2>\n<pre>${JSON.stringify(state, null, 2)}</pre>\n</body></html>`);\n  w.document.close();\n}\n\nfunction deleteScenario() {\n  const sel = el('scenario_select');\n  if (!sel || !sel.value) return;\n  const name = sel.value;\n  if (!confirm(`Delete scenario \"${name}\"?`)) return;\n  const scenarios = _getScenarios();\n  delete scenarios[name];\n  _putScenarios(scenarios);\n  renderScenarioList();\n}\n\n// ─── Initial run\nloadState();\nrenderScenarioList();\nrecalc();"

        "text": "Perfect! Now I have the entire file. Let me perform a comprehensive code review and document my findings.\n\n## Code Review Report: production_spark_calculator.html\n\n### CRITICAL FINDINGS\n\n#### 1. **XSS Vulnerability in viewScenario() — HIGH SEVERITY**\n**Location:** Lines 1280-1288 (viewScenario function)\n\nThe scenario name is passed directly into `document.write()` without escaping:\n```javascript\nw.document.write(`<!DOCTYPE html>...<h2>Scenario JSON — ${sel.value}</title>...`);\n```\n\nIf a user saves a scenario with a name containing HTML/JavaScript (e.g., `<img src=x onerror=\"alert('xss')\"`), it will execute in the new window. The scenario name comes from `window.prompt()` which accepts any user input.\n\n**Impact:** An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in a popup window context. Since scenarios are stored in localStorage, a malicious scenario name could be shared and executed by other users.\n\n**Fix needed:** Escape the scenario name before embedding. Use `textContent` instead of innerHTML, or use proper HTML escaping.\n\n---\n\n#### 2. **XSS in Storage Grid and OSD Visual — MEDIUM SEVERITY**\n**Location:** Lines 1058-1087 (buildStorageGrid, buildOSDVisual functions)\n\nWhile user input doesn't directly flow here, the pattern is dangerous:\n```javascript\ng.innerHTML = items.map(item => `<div...>${item.label}...</div>`).join('');\nv.innerHTML = html;\n```\n\nThe item labels are static in current code, but if ever pulled from user-modifiable data (e.g., VM names), this would be an XSS sink.\n\n---\n\n#### 3. **Param Table XSS — MEDIUM SEVERITY**\n**Location:** Lines 1112-1114 (buildParamTable)\n\n```javascript\nt.innerHTML = rows.map(([k,v,d]) => `<tr><td>${k}</td><td>${v}</td><td>${d}</td></tr>`).join('');\n```\n\nAll parameters are numeric/boolean (safe), but the pattern is risky if descriptions are ever externalized.\n\n---\n\n### WARNING FINDINGS\n\n#### 4. **Pre-Scale Block Dependency Correctness — LOGIC ISSUE**\n**Location:** Lines 781-795 (recalc function, pre-scale block)\n\nThe code correctly loads `ram_node` first in `loadState()` (line 1203) before other range inputs, which ensures the pre-scale block calculates against the correct base `_oldMax`. However:\n\n- **Issue:** The pre-scale block recalculates on every `recalc()` call, even when `ram_node` hasn't changed. On rapid slider movements, this can cause subtle rounding artifacts.\n- **Scenario:** If a user quickly drags the `ram_per_vm` slider, then changes `ram_node`, then drags `ram_per_vm` again, the value may not perfectly rescale if floating-point precision issues occur in line 793:\n  ```javascript\n  const _scaled = Math.round((_min + _pct * (_maxRamVM - _min)) / 32) * 32;\n  ```\n\n- **Current status:** Acceptable since we round to 32 GB steps, but could be more robust.\n\n---\n\n#### 5. **Off-by-One Risk: Executor Ceiling Math**\n**Location:** Lines 897-900\n\n```javascript\nconst EXEC_PER_VM  = Math.max(1, Math.floor(EXEC_VCPUS / EXEC_CORES));\nconst TOTAL_EXEC_RAW = EXEC_PER_VM * TOTAL_VMS;\nconst TOTAL_EXEC = Math.max(1, TOTAL_EXEC_RAW - 1); // 1 for driver\n```\n\nThe `-1` subtracts one executor cluster-wide to account for the Spark driver. However:\n- If `TOTAL_EXEC_RAW = 1`, then `TOTAL_EXEC = 0`, then `Math.max(1, 0) = 1`. This claims 1 executor but if TOTAL_VMS=1 and EXEC_PER_VM=1, there's actually no room for a non-driver executor.\n- **Impact:** Minimal — the warnings check `TOTAL_EXEC < 6` and warn, so this edge case is surfaced. But the math is technically unsound for very small clusters.\n\n---\n\n#### 6. **Division by Zero Risk: Partition Calculation**\n**Location:** Line 914\n\n```javascript\nconst CALC_PARTITIONS = Math.ceil(SHUFFLE_DATA_MB / PART_MB);\n```\n\nIf `PART_MB = 0` (user manipulates slider to 0), this divides by zero → Infinity. The slider has `min=\"64\"`, so this shouldn't occur in the UI. **Status:** Safe in practice, but no defensive check in code.\n\n---\n\n#### 7. **Memory Math: Incorrect Operator Precedence Risk**\n**Location:** Lines 903-908\n\n```javascript\nconst SLOT_GB = EXEC_RAM_VM / EXEC_PER_VM;\nconst MAX_EXEC_MEM = (SLOT_GB - OFFHEAP) / (1 + OVH_PCT);\nconst EXEC_MEM = Math.floor(MAX_EXEC_MEM * 0.95);\nconst ACTUAL_TOTAL = EXEC_MEM * (1 + OVH_PCT) + OFFHEAP;\nconst OOM_MARGIN = SLOT_GB - ACTUAL_TOTAL;\n```\n\nThe formula is correct:\n- Solve: `mem * (1 + overhead) + offheap <= slot`\n- Rearrange: `mem <= (slot - offheap) / (1 + overhead)`\n- Apply 95% safety margin: `mem = floor(max_mem * 0.95)`\n\n**Verification:** For SLOT_GB=64, OFFHEAP=6, OVH_PCT=0.1:\n- MAX_EXEC_MEM = (64-6)/(1.1) = 52.73\n- EXEC_MEM = floor(52.73 * 0.95) = 49\n- ACTUAL_TOTAL = 49 * 1.1 + 6 = 59.9\n- OOM_MARGIN = 64 - 59.9 = 4.1 ✓ (matches warning threshold)\n\n**Status:** Correct.\n\n---\n\n#### 8. **Shuffle Partition Derivation: AQE Logic Flaw**\n**Location:** Lines 911-915\n\n```javascript\nconst DEFAULT_PARALLELISM = TOTAL_EXEC_CORES * 2;\nconst SHUFFLE_DATA_TB = DAILY_TB * SHUF_AMP;\nconst SHUFFLE_DATA_MB = SHUFFLE_DATA_TB * 1024 * 1024;\nconst CALC_PARTITIONS = Math.ceil(SHUFFLE_DATA_MB / PART_MB);\nconst SHUFFLE_PARTS = AQE ? Math.max(2000, CALC_PARTITIONS) : CALC_PARTITIONS;\n```\n\n**Issue:** When AQE is enabled, the code enforces a minimum of 2000 partitions:\n```javascript\nSHUFFLE_PARTS = AQE ? Math.max(2000, CALC_PARTITIONS) : CALC_PARTITIONS;\n```\n\nThis means if SHUFFLE_DATA_MB = 100,000 MB (100 GB) and PART_MB = 200:\n- CALC_PARTITIONS = 500\n- With AQE: SHUFFLE_PARTS = max(2000, 500) = 2000 (50× more partitions than data justifies)\n\n**Real-world impact:** AQE will coalesce these down, so the setting is correct (it's the \"starting ceiling\"). However, the comment on line 1104 says \"AQE will coalesce down\", which is correct. **Status:** Logic is sound; comment is accurate.\n\n---\n\n#### 9. **Ceph Usable Capacity: Replication Math Correctness**\n**Location:** Lines 922-924\n\n```javascript\nconst CEPH_RAW_CLUSTER = OSD_NODE * NVME_TB * NODES;\nconst CEPH_USABLE = CEPH_RAW_CLUSTER / CEPH_REP;\nconst CEPH_DAYS = CEPH_USABLE / DAILY_TB;\n```\n\n**Verification:** With OSD_NODE=4, NVME_TB=3.2, NODES=3, CEPH_REP=3:\n- CEPH_RAW_CLUSTER = 4 * 3.2 * 3 = 38.4 TB\n- CEPH_USABLE = 38.4 / 3 = 12.8 TB ✓ (correct: raw ÷ replication factor)\n- CEPH_DAYS = 12.8 / 2 = 6.4 days ✓\n\n**Status:** Correct.\n\n---\n\n#### 10. **Scratch Allocation Edge Case**\n**Location:** Lines 918-920\n\n```javascript\nconst SCRATCH_NVME_PER_NODE = NVME_COUNT - OSD_NODE;  // 9 - 4 = 5\nconst SCRATCH_NVME_PER_VM = VMS_NODE > 0 ? SCRATCH_NVME_PER_NODE / VMS_NODE : 0;\nconst SCRATCH_TB_PER_VM = SCRATCH_NVME_PER_VM * NVME_TB;\n```\n\n**Issue:** If VMS_NODE = 0 (user sets it to 0), `SCRATCH_NVME_PER_VM = 0`. The slider `min=\"1\"`, so this shouldn't happen in the UI. However, no defensive check in code.\n\n**Additional issue (line 1055):** In buildStorageGrid, the loop variable `i` is compared to `osdCount + scratchNvme`, but:\n```javascript\nfor(let i=osdCount+1; i<=osdCount+scratchNvme; i++) {\n  const vmIdx = (i - osdCount - 1) % vmsNode + 1;\n```\n\nIf `vmsNode = 0`, this causes modulo by zero → NaN. **Status:** Minor, UI enforces min=1, but no defensive check.\n\n---\n\n#### 11. **Warning Logic: Contradiction Between Tests and Messages**\n**Location:** Lines 1141-1148\n\n```javascript\nif(w.NUMA_PIN && w.VMS_NODE === 1 && w.VCPU_VM <= 32) {\n  warnings.push({ type:'info', ... 'Consider 2 VMs per node' ... });\n}\nif(!w.NUMA_PIN) {\n  warnings.push({ type:'warning', ... });\n}\n```\n\n**Issue:** The first check requires `w.NUMA_PIN === true`, then immediately the second check warns if `!w.NUMA_PIN`. So both warnings can't fire at once. This is logically correct but slightly confusing because the condition precedence means:\n- If NUMA_PIN is true AND VMS_NODE=1: show \"Consider 2 VMs\" (info)\n- If NUMA_PIN is false: show \"NUMA pinning disabled\" (warning)\n\nThese are mutually exclusive by design, so no bug. **Status:** Correct.\n\n---\n\n#### 12. **Hidden HTML Element Risk: Missing Scenario in List**\n**Location:** Lines 1234-1236\n\n```javascript\nsel.innerHTML = names.length\n  ? names.map(n => `<option value=\"${n}\">${n}</option>`).join('')\n  : '<option value=\"\" disabled selected>— no saved scenarios —</option>';\n```\n\nNo HTML escaping of scenario names in the option labels. If a scenario name contains `<`, `>`, or quotes, the HTML will break. Since names come from `window.prompt()`, an attacker could save `\"foo\" onload=\"alert('xss')` as a name. The `value` and option text would be malformed, but the prompt-based input is the primary XSS vector (found earlier).\n\n---\n\n#### 13. **State Restoration: Type Coercion Bug**\n**Location:** Lines 1208, 1266\n\n```javascript\nif (state[inp.id] !== undefined) inp.checked = (state[inp.id] === true || state[inp.id] === 'true');\n```\n\nThis checks for both `true` (boolean) and `'true'` (string). However, JSON serialization always produces booleans, so the `=== 'true'` branch can never fire. The code is defensive but unnecessary. **Status:** Safe, no bug.\n\n---\n\n### INFO / BEST PRACTICE FINDINGS\n\n#### 14. **No Input Validation on Range Sliders**\nWhile the HTML specifies `min`, `max`, and `step`, there's no JavaScript validation. An attacker could use browser dev tools to set invalid values. The app should validate on `recalc()`:\n```javascript\nif (+el('daily_tb').value < 1 || +el('daily_tb').value > 8) { /* error */ }\n```\n\n**Current status:** Low risk since the app is single-file, client-side; no backend at risk.\n\n---\n\n#### 15. **NUMA Pinning Warning Incomplete**\n**Location:** Line 1137\n\n```javascript\nif(w.VCPU_VM > 32) {\n  warnings.push({ type:'error', icon:'✕', title:'NUMA boundary crossed', ... });\n}\n```\n\nThe warning triggers if vCPU > 32, but doesn't check if NUMA pinning is enabled. The warning text says \"Keep vCPUs ≤ 32 with NUMA pinning enabled,\" implying NUMA pinning is the solution. However, the warning fires regardless of NUMA_PIN status. **Status:** Minor UX issue; correct behavior but could be clearer.\n\n---\n\n#### 16. **Hardcoded Constants Not Centralized**\n**Location:** Lines 837-843\n\nHardware specs are hardcoded:\n```javascript\nconst NODES = 3;\nconst CORES_NODE = 64;\nconst NVME_COUNT = 9;\nconst NVME_TB = 3.2;\n```\n\nIf the hardware spec changes (e.g., upgrade to 8 nodes), the entire calculator needs code changes. Consider externalizing these to the top of the script. **Status:** Not a bug, but reduces maintainability.\n\n---\n\n#### 17. **localStorage Errors Silently Caught**\n**Location:** Lines 1195, 1210, 1214, 1227\n\nAll localStorage operations are wrapped in `try/catch(e) {}` with no logging. If localStorage is disabled or full, users won't know their saves failed. Consider user feedback: `if(!localStorage) { alert('localStorage unavailable'); }`\n\n**Status:** Acceptable for a calculator, but silent failures are suboptimal UX.\n\n---\n\n#### 18. **loadState() Doesn't Validate Loaded Values**\n**Location:** Lines 1198-1211\n\nAfter loading state from localStorage, the values are set but not validated. If corrupted data exists (e.g., `ram_node: \"abc\"`), the slider value becomes `\"abc\"`, and `+el('ram_node').value` would be `NaN` on next recalc. Add validation:\n```javascript\nif (!/^\\d+$/.test(state.ram_node)) { delete state.ram_node; }\n```\n\n**Status:** Unlikely to occur (JSON.parse would reject non-JSON), but possible if localStorage was manually edited.\n\n---\n\n#### 19. **CSS: Undefined Variables Risk**\n**Location:** Lines 9-26 (CSS variables)\n\nAll CSS variables are defined, and all are used. However, there's no fallback for older browsers that don't support `var()`. On IE11 or old Safari, the calculator would be unstyled. **Status:** Acceptable for a modern production tool; documented as browser-dependent.\n\n---\n\n#### 20. **Safari Range Slider Compatibility**\n**Location:** Lines 137-147\n\nThe code uses `-webkit-appearance: none` for custom range slider styling, which covers both Chrome and Safari. However, Firefox uses a different pseudo-element (`::-moz-range-thumb`), which is not styled here. On Firefox, the slider will have default styling (not matching the dark theme). **Status:** Minor visual inconsistency; functionality is unaffected.\n\n---\n\n#### 21. **Missing Null Checks in DOM Updates**\n**Location:** Throughout `recalc()` (lines 798-1010)\n\nMany calls to `setVal()` and `barWidth()` include null checks:\n```javascript\nfunction setVal(id, v) { const e = el(id); if(e) e.textContent = v; }\n```\n\nThis is good defensive programming. However, some operations like `el('ram_per_vm').max = ...` (line 792) don't check if the element exists. If the HTML is malformed, this will throw. **Status:** Low risk; same file guarantees HTML exists.\n\n---\n\n#### 22. **Executor Math: Driver Exclusion Assumption**\n**Location:** Line 899\n\n```javascript\nconst TOTAL_EXEC = Math.max(1, TOTAL_EXEC_RAW - 1); // 1 for driver\n```\n\nThe code assumes the driver runs in its own executor slot and should be subtracted. In reality, Spark drivers can run in-cluster (on a YARN container) or client-side. The calculator doesn't distinguish. If running driver client-side, all `TOTAL_EXEC_RAW` executors are available. **Status:** Assumption is reasonable for YARN cluster mode (which is configured in spark-defaults.conf, line 1096), but documentation would help.\n\n---\n\n#### 23. **Hidden Storage Row Toggle**\n**Location:** Line 1047\n\nThe services VM row (`ohd_svc_row`) is hidden/shown based on `svc_vm_enabled`:\n```javascript\nif(svcRow) svcRow.style.display = el('svc_vm_enabled').checked ? '' : 'none';\n```\n\nIf a user toggles the checkbox off, the row disappears but is still part of the table. On small screens, this could cause layout shift. **Status:** Minor UX issue; acceptable.\n\n---\n\n### SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| Severity | Category | Count | Status |\n|----------|----------|-------|--------|\n| **CRITICAL** | XSS vulnerabilities | 2 | Scenario name in viewScenario(); param table/storage grid HTML injection sinks |\n| **WARNING** | Logic/edge cases | 6 | Pre-scale rounding; executor ceiling off-by-one; division by zero; partition ceiling; division by zero (modulo); hidden state toggle |\n| **INFO** | Best practices | 15 | Input validation, Firefox slider styling, localStorage UX, hardcoded constants, nullable DOM elements, etc. |\n\n### RECOMMENDATION PRIORITY\n\n1. **Immediate:** Fix XSS in `viewScenario()` by escaping scenario name before `document.write()`.\n2. **High:** Review `buildParamTable()` and `buildStorageGrid()` for XSS sinks if data sources ever become user-modifiable.\n3. **Medium:** Add defensive checks for edge cases (VMS_NODE=0, PART_MB=0, division by zero in modulo).\n4. **Low:** Improve UX (localStorage error feedback, Firefox slider styling, hardcoded constant documentation).\n\nAll mathematical formulas (executor memory, Ceph capacity, partition sizing, OOM margins) are **correct** and properly derived from the Spark/YARN/Ceph architecture."
      }
    ]
  }
]

    "text": "Here are the findings from the full code review. No changes have been made.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: `production_spark_calculator.html`\n\n*(The dev calculator shares the same logic; findings apply to both.)*\n\n---\n\n### Critical\n\n**1. XSS in `viewScenario()` — scenario name injected into `document.write()` unescaped**\n\nLocation: `viewScenario()` function.\n\nThe scenario name from `window.prompt()` is embedded directly into the HTML written to the popup window:\n```js\nw.document.write(`...<h2>Scenario JSON — ${sel.value}</h2>...`);\n```\nA name containing `<script>` or an event attribute would execute in the popup context. Since scenarios are stored in localStorage, a crafted name persists across sessions.\n\n**Fix:** HTML-escape the scenario name before embedding, e.g.:\n```js\nconst safeName = sel.value.replace(/&/g,'&amp;').replace(/</g,'&lt;').replace(/>/g,'&gt;');\n```\n\n**2. Scenario names unescaped in dropdown `<option>` elements**\n\nLocation: `renderScenarioList()`.\n\nSame issue — scenario names are interpolated directly into `innerHTML`. A name containing `\"` or `>` would break the HTML structure.\n\n---\n\n### Warnings\n\n**3. Executor driver subtraction is technically unsound at minimum cluster size**\n\nLocation: `const TOTAL_EXEC = Math.max(1, TOTAL_EXEC_RAW - 1)`\n\nIf `TOTAL_EXEC_RAW = 1` (single executor per entire cluster), the result is clamped to 1 but there is genuinely no room for both a driver and a worker executor. The warning threshold (`TOTAL_EXEC < 6`) surfaces this, but the reported count is misleading.\n\n**4. NUMA boundary warning ignores NUMA pinning state**\n\nLocation: `if(w.VCPU_VM > 32)` warning block.\n\nThe warning fires regardless of whether NUMA pinning is enabled. The message says \"keep vCPUs ≤ 32 with NUMA pinning enabled,\" implying pinning is the remedy — but if pinning is already off, the fix is different. The warning could be more specific to the actual state.\n\n**5. Driver deployment mode assumption undocumented**\n\nLocation: `TOTAL_EXEC_RAW - 1` driver deduction.\n\nThe calculator assumes `--deploy-mode cluster`, where the driver occupies one executor slot on the cluster. This is correctly reflected in the generated `spark-defaults.conf` output, but no comment or warning explains that if the driver were run client-side, all `TOTAL_EXEC_RAW` slots would be available for workers.\n\n**6. Scratch drive warning threshold is loose**\n\nLocation: `if(w.SCRATCH_TB_PER_VM < w.DAILY_TB * w.SHUF_AMP * 0.5)`\n\nThe warning only fires when scratch is below 50% of worst-case shuffle volume. At that point a shuffle spill to Ceph S3 is not just possible but likely. A tighter threshold (e.g., 75–80%) would give earlier notice.\n\n**7. `loadState()` and `loadScenario()` do not validate restored values**\n\nIf localStorage contains corrupted or manually-edited data, a non-numeric string set as a slider value causes `+el(...).value` to return `NaN` in `recalc()`, silently poisoning all downstream calculations with no visible error. The issue is unlikely in normal use but has no guard.\n\n**8. Silent `catch(e) {}` on all localStorage operations**\n\nIf localStorage is unavailable (private browsing in some browsers, quota exceeded) saves fail silently. The user sees the \"Saved ✓\" feedback but nothing was actually written.\n\n---\n\n### Correct — Verified\n\nThe following were carefully checked and found to be arithmetically correct:\n\n| Formula | Verdict |\n|---|---|\n| Executor memory: `(SLOT_GB - OFFHEAP) / (1 + OVH_PCT) × 0.95` | Correct |\n| OOM margin: `SLOT_GB - (EXEC_MEM × (1 + OVH_PCT) + OFFHEAP)` | Correct |\n| Ceph usable: `OSD_NODE × NVME_TB × NODES / CEPH_REP` | Correct |\n| Available RAM/cores after reservations | Correct |\n| RAM/VM pre-scale proportional rescaling | Correct |\n| AQE partition floor of 2000 (starting ceiling, coalesced down) | Correct and documented |\n| `spark.default.parallelism = TOTAL_EXEC_CORES × 2` | Correct |\n| Services VM amortized across 3 nodes with `Math.ceil` | Correct |\n| Scratch sufficiency check | Correct (threshold conservatism noted above) |\n\n---\n\n### Info / Best Practice\n\n- **Firefox:** Range slider thumb uses only `-webkit-*` pseudo-elements. Firefox renders default styling — functional but visually inconsistent with the dark theme.\n- **Hardcoded hardware constants** (`NODES`, `CORES_NODE`, `NVME_COUNT`, `NVME_TB`) are scattered mid-function rather than grouped at the top of the script, making a hardware refresh harder to audit.\n- **`state[inp.id] === 'true'` branch** in checkbox restoration can never fire — JSON always deserializes booleans as booleans, not strings. Harmless but dead code.\n- **No defensive guard on `el('ram_per_vm').max = ...`** — if the element were missing the script would throw. Low risk since HTML and JS are in the same file, but worth noting.\n\n---\n\n### Priority Order for Fixes\n\n| Priority | Item |\n|---|---|\n| 1 | XSS in `viewScenario()` — escape scenario name |\n| 2 | XSS in `renderScenarioList()` — escape option text and value |\n| 3 | localStorage failure feedback to user |\n| 4 | NUMA warning specificity |\n| 5 | Scratch threshold tightening |\n| 6 | Hardcoded constants consolidation |"
  }
]

[
  {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "A few options depending on what format you need:\n\n**Within Claude Code (easiest)**\nThe full conversation transcript is stored at:\n```\n/Users/rohn/.claude/projects/-Users-rohn-Serve/90e862e6-df42-4e2e-84b3-331f52c3e391.jsonl\n```\nThat's the raw JSONL file. It contains every message in the conversation including tool calls and results.\n\n**Export as readable text**\nYou can convert it to plain text by running in your terminal:\n```\n! cat /Users/rohn/.claude/projects/-Users-rohn-Serve/90e862e6-df42-4e2e-84b3-331f52c3e391.jsonl\n```\nOr pipe it through `jq` if you have it installed for cleaner formatting:\n```\n! jq -r '.message.content' /Users/rohn/.claude/projects/-Users-rohn-Serve/90e862e6-df42-4e2e-84b3-381f52c3e391.jsonl 2>/dev/null\n```\n\n**From the browser (claude.ai)**\nIf you're also using Claude via the web interface, the conversation may be accessible there and you can use the browser's print-to-PDF function.\n\n**Note on the JSONL file:** This conversation was summarized mid-session due to context length, so the very early turns (the initial calculator examination) are in the summary rather than as raw messages. The JSONL file will reflect that — the summary plus everything from this session."
  }

If I run the jq version where does the output go?
null
[
  {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "It prints to the terminal — stdout. To save it to a file in your `~/Serve` directory:\n\n```\n! jq -r '.message.content' /Users/rohn/.claude/projects/-Users-rohn-Serve/90e862e6-df42-4e2e-84b3-331f52c3e391.jsonl > /Users/rohn/Serve/chat_export.txt 2>/dev/null\n```\n\nThe `>` redirects stdout to the file. The `2>/dev/null` discards any jq errors (some lines in the JSONL may not have `.message.content` and jq would complain about those).\n\nThat said, the output may be messy — the JSONL structure isn't designed for human reading, and content fields can contain escaped JSON, tool call results, and metadata mixed in with the actual conversation text. You might end up with something partial or hard to read.\n\nA cleaner option if you just want the narrative for a report: select all text in this Claude Code window and paste into a document. The rendered markdown will copy cleanly."
  }
]
